novel_features.name: CI readiness gate
novel_features.description: Fail a build when a site drops below a target readiness level or when a previously-passing check regresses.
novel_features.rationale: Needs a locally-stored baseline to define 'regressed against last scan' and to tell a target siteError apart from a real readiness drop; the stateless web UI has neither.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this in CI when you want a deterministic exit code, not a brittle grep of the JSON.

novel_features.name: Cross-site open-advice backlog
novel_features.description: List every still-failing check across all your scanned sites with its fix prompt, so you see exactly what is left to do.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the newest stored scan per site and keeps only checks still failing; no single scan call knows your whole estate or which advice you already actioned.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer 'what fixes are still open across all my sites' in one command.

novel_features.name: Score-over-time history
novel_features.description: Show a site's readiness level across every past scan and flag which checks flipped pass/fail between scans.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local time series of scans; the scan API and web UI are stateless and have no history endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to confirm a fix actually landed and to catch silent regressions.

novel_features.name: Two-scan regression diff
novel_features.description: Diff two scans of a site (default: the latest two) into a per-check regressed/fixed/unchanged table plus the level delta.
novel_features.rationale: Pairwise transition tables require two stored scans to compare; the API returns one snapshot at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to see precisely what changed between two points in time, not just the new score.

novel_features.name: Competitor standards matrix
novel_features.description: Scan several sites and print a check-by-check matrix of which agent standards each one implements, plus each site's level.
novel_features.rationale: A side-by-side matrix needs results from multiple sites at once; the web UI scans one URL with no comparison.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to see exactly which standards a competitor implemented that you have not.

novel_features.name: Portfolio batch scan
novel_features.description: Scan a list of URLs from a file or stdin, persist each, and print a leaderboard ranked by level or failing-check count.
novel_features.rationale: Multi-target scan, persistence, and ranking are a local-store loop over the single-target API; the web UI takes one URL per session.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to triage a whole web estate worst-first instead of scanning sites one browser tab at a time.

narrative.headline: The terminal scanner for AI-agent readiness: every check the web tool runs, plus copy-paste fixes, CI gating, scan history, and a local store the web UI has no answer for.
narrative.value_prop: isitagentready.com gives you a one-shot score in a browser tab with no memory. This CLI turns it into a repeatable loop: check any site, get the prioritized fixes with advice, gate it in CI, diff it over time, compare it to competitors, and batch-scan a whole portfolio, with every scan stored locally so history and open-advice tell you exactly what changed and what is still unfixed.
narrative.auth_narrative: No API key or login required. The scan endpoint is public and read-only, so every command works out of the box.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the scanner API is reachable before your first scan.
Scan a site: prints the readiness level and per-category summary, and stores the scan locally.
The headline: prioritized, copy-paste fixes to reach the next readiness level.
Use in CI: exits non-zero if the site is below level 3.

troubleshoots.symptom: Scan returns a siteError block instead of checks
troubleshoots.fix: The TARGET site was unreachable (403/timeout), not the scanner. Confirm the URL loads in a browser; the scanner could not fetch it.

troubleshoots.symptom: advice, history, or open-advice prints nothing
troubleshoots.fix: Run isitagentready-pp-cli check <url> first; those commands read the local scan store.
Normal: the scanner runs many live probes per scan. Use batch to scan many sites in one command.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this when you need to check or improve how ready a website is for AI agents: scanning a site for robots.txt, llms.txt, MCP, OAuth, and commerce-protocol readiness, getting copy-paste fixes to raise its level, gating a deploy on readiness, tracking a site's score over time, or comparing several sites. It is ideal inside a coding agent that will then apply the fixes it surfaces.
anti_triggers: Generating an llms.txt or robots.txt file from scratch (this CLI scans and advises; it does not author those files for you).
Tracking whether ChatGPT or Perplexity cite your brand (that is AI-visibility tracking, a different product).
General SEO keyword-ranking or backlink audits.
Fetching a page's content as markdown for an agent to read (that is the markdown-negotiation feature on the target site, not this scanner).

recipes.title: Get the copy-paste fixes for a site
recipes.explanation: Prints every next-level fix prompt as one pasteable block, ready to drop into a coding agent.

recipes.title: See what is still unfixed across all your sites
recipes.explanation: Cross-site backlog of every check still failing, with its fix prompt, from your local scan history.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose scan to just the checks you care about
recipes.explanation: A full scan is large and deeply nested; --select pulls just the dotted fields you want so an agent does not parse tens of KB.

recipes.title: Gate a deploy on readiness
recipes.explanation: Exits non-zero if the site is below level 3 or any previously-passing check regressed; safe for CI.

recipes.title: Compare against a competitor
recipes.explanation: Prints a per-standard matrix of which agent-readiness checks each site implements.

trigger_phrases: scan my site for agent readiness
how do I make my site ready for AI agents
fix my site for AI agents
check llms.txt and MCP discovery on this site
